content: Mock chaining patterns like mock_db.query().filter().first.return_value fail - need to mock each step: mock_db.query.return_value.filter.return_value.first.return_value
Framework migrations (FastAPI to Flask) should include backend implementation and migration plan, not just template changes that break existing functionality
Test files should not be truncated mid-function or contain only backticks - incomplete files cause syntax errors and break test runs
Base class should be imported from models module, not database module - importing Base from database causes runtime import failures
HTMX forms need CSRF protection with tokens like {{ csrf_token() }} and user content should be escaped with |e filter to prevent XSS
Verify imports and dependencies exist before writing tests — reviewer flagged missing 'create_app' function and non-existent API endpoints like '/api/songs'
Ensure model methods exist before writing tests that depend on them — reviewer noted missing methods like add_song(), remove_song(), reorder_songs(), and clone() on Setlist model
Validate data integrity constraints at the model level — reviewer noted that negative duration validation should be enforced in the model rather than just tested
Mock database query chains properly by setting return_value for each chained method — `mock_db.query.return_value.filter.return_value.first.return_value` instead of incomplete chains that won't work
Write tests after implementing the actual service methods, not before — tests that reference non-existent methods like `_validate_venue_data`, `search_venues` will fail and create confusion
When patching datetime in tests, patch where it's used in the service code, not where it's imported in the test module — incorrect patch locations won't affect the actual code being tested

Always verify file references and dependencies exist before committing — multiple critical issues with missing database.py file referenced in main.py and corrupted package initialization files containing triple backticks
Configure .gitignore properly to exclude build artifacts — Python cache files (__pycache__) should never be committed to git repository
Maintain essential dependencies for framework functionality — removing core dependencies like pytest, httpx, pydantic, and alembic breaks testing and FastAPI capabilities
Always include all referenced files and dependencies in PRs — missing models.py, schemas.py, database.py, and tours.py router caused import errors that would break the application
Implement pagination on GET endpoints that return collections — missing limit/offset parameters on get_concerts() could cause performance issues with large datasets
Write complete test cases that actually validate the functionality being tested — incomplete foreign key constraint test doesn't properly validate constraints and could miss data integrity issues
Never implement complete framework changes without a proper migration plan and ensuring backend compatibility — switching from FastAPI to Flask without implementation breaks the entire application
Always implement security protections including CSRF tokens on forms/HTMX requests and XSS escaping for user content — use Flask-WTF protection and template escaping like {{ content|e }}
Include comprehensive testing and error handling for new functionality — provide test coverage for new features and user feedback mechanisms for HTMX request failures
Verify all imports reference existing modules before submitting — tests imported 'app' and 'models' modules that may not exist, causing potential runtime failures
Implement dependencies before writing dependent code — tests called methods like setlist.add_song() and referenced API endpoints that weren't implemented yet
Ensure code files are complete in diffs — the test file was truncated with an incomplete test method, making review difficult

Mock chaining patterns like `mock_db.query().filter().first.return_value` fail because intermediate mock objects don't automatically chain. Use `configure_mock()` or `spec` parameter to properly set up mock chains, or mock at a higher level to avoid chaining.

Don't write tests for private methods that may not exist in the actual implementation. Tests should focus on public interfaces and behavior, not internal implementation details like `_validate_venue_data()`.

Use consistent mocking patterns throughout test files. Establish a clear approach for mocking database operations and stick to it rather than mixing different mock strategies that have varying reliability.
Always ensure all imported dependencies are created before referencing them — missing database.py file was imported in main.py and models but never created, causing critical build failures
Never commit Python cache files (__pycache__) to version control — add them to .gitignore and remove from repository to keep it clean
Use FastAPI lifespan context manager instead of deprecated @app.on_event('startup') for FastAPI 0.104.1+ — the on_event decorators are being phased out
Verify all imports exist before implementing endpoints — missing imports for Concert, Tour, ConcertCreate, ConcertUpdate, ConcertResponse and database/models modules caused critical failures
Implement proper test isolation with session-scoped fixtures — function-scoped database setup/teardown and cross-test data contamination can cause unreliable tests
Use consistent error message formatting across endpoints — inconsistent casing like 'Tour not found' vs 'Concert not found' reduces code quality
Always verify import statements point to correct modules — Base was imported from wrong module (.database instead of .models) causing critical import errors
Maintain consistent REST API patterns across all endpoints — mixing /api/v1/concerts/{id}/setlist and /api/v1/setlists/{id} patterns breaks API design consistency
Always add __pycache__/ to .gitignore and avoid committing Python cache files to version control — these auto-generated files pollute the repository
Never replace entire technology stacks (FastAPI -> Flask) without coordinating backend implementation and providing clear justification — this breaks existing applications
Always implement backend endpoints before referencing them in frontend code — HTMX endpoints like reorder_setlist, remove_from_setlist will cause 404 errors if not implemented
Include CSRF protection tokens in all POST/DELETE forms for security — all HTMX forms should have CSRF tokens to prevent attacks
Implement foundational layers before dependent components — tests were written for models (Song, Setlist, SetlistSong) and Flask app structure that don't exist yet, causing import failures
Create model methods before writing tests that use them — tests reference methods like add_song(), remove_song(), reorder_songs(), and clone() that need to be implemented first
Add model-level validation for data integrity constraints — allowing negative duration values could cause data issues, validation should exist at the model layer not just forms/API

Mock chained method calls require explicit return_value assignment at each level — use mock_db.query.return_value.filter.return_value.first.return_value instead of assuming mock_db.query().filter().first() will work
Always verify that referenced methods and imports exist before writing tests — tests should not reference non-existent methods like _validate_venue_data or missing module paths
Test public interface methods rather than private methods — avoid testing private methods like _validate_venue_data directly and instead test them through public methods
Always ensure all imported modules exist before creating imports — database.py was imported but missing from the PR, causing critical import errors
Never commit Python cache files (__pycache__/) or binary files to git — add these to .gitignore to keep repository clean
Verify all required dependencies are in requirements.txt before removing them — essential packages like pydantic, pytest, httpx were removed despite being used in code

Always verify imports exist before using them — critical failures occurred from importing undefined modules (..models, ..schemas, ..database, .routers.tours) that prevented the application from starting
Implement core dependencies before creating dependent code — the tours router and database models need to be created before they can be imported and used in main application files
Use specific error handling instead of generic catch-all — replace generic IntegrityError handling with specific error messages for different constraint violations (unique, foreign key, etc.)
Always include all dependent files in PRs — importing models/modules that aren't included in the PR (like venue.py and database.py) causes import errors and breaks functionality
Exclude build artifacts from version control — Python cache files (.pyc) should be added to .gitignore and not committed to the repository

Use proper object relationships in tests instead of hardcoded values — integration tests with hardcoded IDs (venue_id=1, concert_id=1) will fail in real database scenarios and should use actual foreign key relationships or mocks
Always verify import paths match actual module structure — 'Base' should be imported from models module, not database module, to avoid runtime failures
Never use CORS allow_origins=['*'] in production code — either use specific origins or make CORS configuration environment-dependent for security
Ensure all endpoint parameters are defined in function signatures — query parameters like song_id must be included in the function definition to avoid runtime errors
When changing frameworks (FastAPI to Flask), ensure all dependent code is updated consistently — templates used Flask url_for() but no Flask app existed, creating broken references
Always implement CSRF protection for state-changing operations — all HTMX POST/DELETE requests lacked CSRF tokens, creating security vulnerabilities
Verify HTMX target selectors match actual DOM elements — used '#setlist-container' selectors that didn't match real container IDs, breaking partial updates
Always verify that imported modules and classes exist before writing tests — importing VenueService from src.services.venue_service when the file/class doesn't exist will cause import failures

When mocking chained database operations, set up return_value for each intermediate call in the chain — mock_db.query().filter().first() needs return_value on both query() and filter() methods, not just the final method
Include at least one integration test that doesn't mock everything to verify the service works with real dependencies — testing only with mocks doesn't catch integration issues
Ensure all files are complete before committing — multiple files contained only backticks and comments, breaking the package structure and missing critical imports
Never commit Python cache files (__pycache__) to version control — add them to .gitignore instead to keep repository clean
Verify all dependencies are included in requirements.txt before removing them — essential packages like pytest, pydantic, and alembic were removed despite being used in the code
Always verify all imported modules exist before implementing — missing imports for models.py, schemas.py, and database.py caused critical review failures
Ensure exact string matching in test assertions — inconsistent case checking ('tour not found' vs 'Tour not found') causes test failures
Write definitive test assertions or remove flaky tests — unclear expectations in database-dependent tests lead to unreliable test suites

Always verify import paths are correct and follow project structure — broke imports with 'from src.database import Base, engine' instead of using relative imports like 'from .database import engine' and 'from .models import Base'
Never remove existing functionality without explicit justification — removed tours router and all existing schema imports without explanation, breaking dependent functionality
Add input validation for data uniqueness constraints — should validate venue uniqueness by name/address to prevent duplicates, either through database constraints or application validation
Always include all dependent files in PRs — importing models or modules that aren't included in the PR causes import errors and breaks functionality
Use proper foreign key relationships in tests instead of hardcoded IDs — hardcoded values don't test actual relationships and will fail with real database constraints
Add __pycache__/ and *.pyc to .gitignore — compiled Python files should never be committed to version control
Always verify import statements match actual module structure — importing Base from wrong module (.database vs .models) and missing router imports will cause runtime failures
Ensure function signatures match endpoint parameter expectations — query parameters used in endpoint logic must be explicitly defined in function signature with proper type annotations
Maintain API documentation quality by preserving docstrings and metadata — removing version, description, and docstrings from FastAPI apps reduces usability and maintainability
Never change frameworks without implementing the complete backend — switching from FastAPI to Flask requires implementing all routes, models, and app setup before the templates will function
Always implement referenced routes before updating templates — templates using url_for() for Flask routes like 'reorder_setlist', 'add_to_setlist' will break if the backend routes don't exist
Include CSRF protection on all HTMX POST/DELETE operations — HTMX endpoints are vulnerable to cross-site request forgery attacks without proper token validation
When mocking chained method calls, use `mock_obj.method.return_value.next_method.return_value` syntax instead of `mock_obj.method().next_method()` to properly set up the mock chain behavior
Verify that all methods referenced in tests actually exist in the implementation — avoid testing private methods that may not be implemented or use incorrect naming conventions
When patching imports in tests, verify the exact import path used in the actual implementation rather than assuming the module structure — incorrect patch paths will cause mocks to fail silently
Ensure all files contain proper code content before committing — multiple files contained only triple backticks instead of actual Python code, causing critical functionality gaps

Verify all imported dependencies exist before removing them from requirements — removing essential dependencies like alembic, psycopg2-binary, pydantic causes import errors and breaks core functionality
Add Python cache directories (__pycache__/) to .gitignore to prevent committing generated files to version control
Always verify all imports exist before implementing features — missing Concert, Tour models and database configuration caused critical failures
Establish foundational database and model infrastructure before building dependent API endpoints and tests
Write specific test assertions that validate exact behavior rather than generic error catching that may mask different failure types
Quality gate 'e2e_tests' failed — {"total": 6, "passed": 0, "failed": 6, "status": "fail"}
Quality gate 'coverage' failed — {"percent": 0.0, "threshold": 70, "status": "fail"}
